verdict_reason: Chapter 2 thesis: refuse the tactical answer, reframe at first principles. 2.42 anchors in the karma-yoga thread by naming the obstruction that prevents resolved buddhi from establishing — partial reading of available evidence as if it were the totality. The engineering layer preserves the chapter's emphasis on action-from-grounded-substrate by naming what blocks that grounding (closure-clause partial readings). Verse ordering: 2.41 names the precondition (resolved buddhi), 2.42-43 name the obstruction (kāmya-vāda fixation with closure clause), 2.44 names the consequence (samādhi cannot form), 2.45 names the imperative (rise above guṇas). 2.42's place in this progression is the diagnosis. The block builds correctly. PASS for both 10.3 and 10.4.
